sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Analysts’ Opinions Are Mixed on These Industrial Goods Stocks: American Airlines (AAL), Great Lakes Dredge & Dock (GLDD) and Waste Management (WM)
feed.summary: This article presents a mixed review of analyst opinions on three industrial goods stocks: American Airlines (AAL), Great Lakes Dredge & Dock (GLDD), and Waste Management (WM). While American Airlines has a Moderate Buy consensus with some analysts maintaining a Hold, both Great Lakes Dredge & Dock and Waste Management have stronger Buy ratings from analysts, indicating positive outlooks for these two companies. Each stock's current price, price target, and analyst success rates are detailed.

feed.title: Hennessy Advisors, Inc. (NASDAQ:HNNA) Sees Significant Decrease in Short Interest
feed.summary: Hennessy Advisors, Inc. (NASDAQ:HNNA) experienced a significant 40.6% decrease in short interest in January, totaling 5,595 shares as of January 15th, resulting in a very low days-to-cover ratio of 0.2 days. Institutional investment saw growth, with firms like Truffle Hound Capital LLC and Navellier & Associates Inc. increasing their stakes. The company's stock currently trades around $10.09 with a market capitalization of $79.59 million and a P/E ratio of 8.00, while Weiss Ratings reissued a "hold (c+)" rating.

feed.title: First Hawaiian Bank (NASDAQ:FHB) Reports Q4 CY2025 In Line With Expectations
feed.summary: First Hawaiian Bank (NASDAQ:FHB) reported Q4 CY2025 results that were largely in line with analyst expectations, with revenue up 5.4% year-on-year to $225.9 million and GAAP EPS beating estimates by 2%. The bank's net interest income was in line with predictions, while its efficiency ratio and tangible book value per share exceeded expectations. Despite flat stock performance immediately after the announcement, the results indicate a decent quarter for the Hawaiian banking company.

feed.title: PC Connection improves EcoVadis score 25 percent earning Bronze medal
feed.summary: PC Connection has earned an EcoVadis Bronze medal, demonstrating a 25% year-over-year improvement in their sustainability score. This places the company in the top 35% of all businesses assessed by EcoVadis, a trusted sustainability rating service. The achievement highlights PC Connection's commitment to corporate responsibility, aligning with its strategic vision and innovative technology efforts like the AI webinar with Microsoft 365 Copilot.

feed.title: Lendway Rebrands as Bloomia Holdings with New Ticker
feed.summary: Lendway, Inc. has officially rebranded as Bloomia Holdings, Inc., effective January 28, 2026, with its stock beginning to trade under the new Nasdaq ticker "TULP" on February 2, 2026. This change reflects the company's strategic shift towards its tulip business and aligns its market identity with its core operations as one of the largest producers of fresh-cut tulips in the U.S. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, currently rates LDWY as Neutral due to weak financial performance but notes a recent financing as a potential positive.

feed.title: Lendway (LDWY) to Rebrand as Bloomia Holdings, Ticker to Change to TULP
feed.summary: Lendway Inc. (LDWY) is rebranding to Bloomia Holdings, with its ticker symbol changing to "TULP" around February 2nd on the Nasdaq Capital Market. This strategic move emphasizes the company's focus on its tulip business, seen as its most valuable enterprise, aligning its identity with this core operation. Despite this rebrand, financial analysis of Lendway reveals significant concerns including negative margins, high leverage, and a distressed Altman Z-Score, indicative of potential financial instability.

feed.title: Caledonia Announces Closing of Upsized $150 Million Convertible Senior Notes Offering and Full Exercise of Initial Purchasers' Option to Purchase Additional Notes
feed.summary: Caledonia Mining Corporation Plc has announced the closing of its upsized $150 million Convertible Senior Notes offering, including the full exercise of the initial purchasers' option to buy additional notes. The offering, managed by Cantor Fitzgerald & Co., received an outstanding response from institutional investors, enabling Caledonia to secure long-term capital to further its growth objectives. The notes, with a 5.875% cash interest coupon, will mature on January 15, 2033, and have a conversion price of approximately $40.51 per common share, representing a 25% premium.

feed.title: Footwear giant Genesco on hunt for new CFO
feed.summary: Footwear giant Genesco, parent company of Journeys, announced that CFO Cassandra “Sandra” Harris is stepping down effective March 6 to pursue other opportunities. CEO Mimi E. Vaughn, who previously served as CFO, will assume the interim role while the company searches for a permanent replacement. Genesco is seeking a strategic leader to continue driving shareholder value for its brands, which also include Little Burgundy, Schuh, and Johnston & Murphy.

feed.title: Oglethorpe Power Picks Kiewit Unit as EPC for $2.5B Gas-Fired Georgia Power Plant
feed.summary: Oglethorpe Power has selected Kiewit subsidiary The Industrial Co. (TIC) as the EPC contractor for a new 1.4-GW combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, Georgia. The plant, estimated to cost between $2 billion and $3 billion, will utilize GE Vernova 7HA.03 advanced-class gas turbines capable of operating with a blended hydrogen-natural gas fuel and will feature air-cooled condensers to reduce water use. Commercial operation is targeted for 2029, with the project designed to meet increasing electricity demand and strengthen regional energy resources.

feed.title: Research Alert: CFRA Reiterates Sell View On Shares Of Nvr, Inc.
feed.summary: CFRA has reiterated its "Sell" view on shares of NVR, Inc. The alert also briefly mentions Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results, reporting a revenue increase to $609 million, beating estimates, but noting a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: Does AI-Sovereign Nodes And High-Density Demand Reshape The Bull Case For Equinix (EQIX)?
feed.summary: Equinix is seeing renewed interest due to major bank support and AI-focused customers deploying high-density and sovereign AI infrastructure in its data centers, like the new sovereign AI node in Australia. This development reinforces Equinix's role in performance, security, and data residency, potentially leading to stronger bookings and pricing power in high-density AI capacity. However, investors should be mindful of its rich earnings multiple, high debt, leadership transitions, and the amplified risk of disappointment from its premium valuation.

feed.title: Boston Scientific’s Nalu And Penumbra Bets Reframe Growth And Valuation Profile
feed.summary: Boston Scientific recently acquired Nalu Medical and announced plans to acquire Penumbra, significantly shifting its portfolio towards high-growth medical technology segments like pain management and neurovascular therapies. These strategic moves are expected to reframe Boston Scientific's growth and valuation profile, but investors should monitor the integration process, associated costs, and regulatory approvals. The company's valuation is flagged as trading above estimated fair value, with one risk around debt levels due to these transactions.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser (WY) Premium 57x P/E Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narratives
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (WY) recently concluded FY 2025 with mixed financial results, posting quarterly revenue of US$1,541 million and an EPS of US$0.10 for Q4 2025. Despite reported net income of US$324 million, the company's net margin softened to 4.7%, raising concerns about weaker profitability. The stock trades at a premium 57.3x P/E, significantly higher than its industry average, which is supported by analyst consensus for strong earnings growth but modest revenue growth, alongside dividend coverage concerns.

feed.title: First Hawaiian (FHB) Net Margin Improvement Challenges Flat Long Term Earnings Narrative
feed.summary: First Hawaiian (FHB) reported strong Q3 FY 2025 results with increased revenue and EPS, showcasing improved net profit margins and net interest margins. While recent profitability is strong, the bank's five-year profit growth has been flat, raising questions about the sustainability of these recent gains. The stock's valuation shows a split, with its P/E ratio near industry averages but a significantly higher DCF fair value, indicating potential undervaluation by the market relative to model-based estimates.

feed.title: Blue Foundry Bancorp shareholders approve merger with Fulton Financial
feed.summary: Shareholders of Blue Foundry Bancorp (NASDAQ:BLFY) have approved a merger with Fulton Financial Corporation. The agreement, originally signed on November 24, 2025, will see Blue Foundry Bancorp merge into Fulton Financial, with Fulton as the surviving entity. This acquisition, valued at approximately $243 million, aims to expand Fulton’s presence in northern New Jersey.

feed.title: Huntsman Corp. downgraded to ’BB’ by S&P on weak credit metrics
feed.summary: S&P Global Ratings has downgraded Huntsman Corp. to ’BB’ from ’BB+’ with a negative outlook, citing persistently weak credit metrics expected through 2026 due to high inflation, elevated interest rates, and global challenges. The agency projects modest improvement in 2025 and 2026, supported by cost-reduction efforts and easing U.S. mortgage rates, but warns of further downgrades if EBITDA remains low or debt-funded acquisitions occur.

feed.title: Colorado lawmakers bombard Xcel Energy President with questions, concerns about Public Safety Power Shutoffs
feed.summary: Colorado lawmakers held a nearly five-hour hearing questioning Xcel Energy President Robert Kenney on the company's Public Safety Power Shutoffs (PSPS), which are implemented to prevent wildfires but have caused significant disruption and financial loss to residents and businesses. Lawmakers expressed concerns about communication, litigation avoidance, and financial compensation for those affected. Kenney defended the PSPS as a necessary tool for wildfire mitigation, while acknowledging efforts to improve their implementation and precision.

feed.title: Robert Bender&Associates Trims Apple Inc Stake Significantly
feed.summary: Robert Bender&Associates, a hedge fund managed by Robert L. Bender, has significantly reduced its stake in Apple Inc (AAPL) by selling 5,824 shares. Despite recent volatility, Apple's stock maintains a "Moderate Buy" rating from Wall Street, with analysts projecting solid upside due to strong iPhone revenue, expanding product margins, and growth in services. TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates AAPL an "Outperform" based on strong financials and earnings outlook, though acknowledging mixed near-term technical momentum and a premium valuation.

feed.title: Saybrook Capital /Nc trims Alphabet Inc. Class A stake
feed.summary: Saybrook Capital /Nc, managed by Luke M. Babcock & Scott R. Hirsch, recently reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL) by 1,048 shares. Despite this trim, Alphabet's stock has seen significant gains, outperforming the NASDAQ and its FANG peers, with Wall Street analysts maintaining a Strong Buy consensus due to its strong AI position and financial performance.

feed.title: Robert Bender&Associates cuts Alphabet Inc. Class C stake
feed.summary: Robert Bender&Associates, a hedge fund managed by Robert L. Bender, recently reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. Class C (GOOG) by 1,468 shares. Despite this sale, Alphabet's stock has seen strong rallies, and Wall Street analysts maintain a Strong Buy consensus, highlighting the company's AI strategy and Google Cloud Platform as key growth drivers. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, also rates GOOG as Outperform due to its strong financial performance and strategic investments.

feed.title: The Truth About PepsiCo Inc: Why Everyone Is Suddenly Paying Attention
feed.summary: PepsiCo Inc. is garnering significant attention online due to its diverse product portfolio, spanning beverages and snacks, which consistently generates viral content on platforms like TikTok and YouTube. The company's strategy involves leveraging its various brands (Pepsi, Doritos, Gatorade, etc.) to engage with youth culture through challenges and collaborations, fueling continuous social media trends. From an investment perspective, PEP is considered a stable, defensive stock known for steady cash flow and dividends rather than rapid growth, making it an appealing option for long-term consumer exposure despite snack inflation.

feed.title: The Truth About PepsiCo Inc: Why Everyone Is Suddenly Paying Attention
feed.summary: PepsiCo Inc. is making waves online due to its diverse portfolio of beverages and snacks consistently going viral on platforms like TikTok and YouTube. The article highlights that PepsiCo is an "ecosystem" beyond just cola, with brands like Lay's and Doritos feeding into social media trends and challenges. While the stock (PEP) is considered a stable, defensive investment, its products maintain cultural relevance and perceived value despite rising prices, making it a strong contender in both consumer and investment spheres.

feed.title: Boeing reaches labor deal with former Spirit AeroSystems white-collar workers
feed.summary: Boeing has successfully negotiated a new contract with approximately 1,600 white-collar workers from the former Spirit AeroSystems, which it re-acquired in December. The deal, approved by 85% of votes, includes a $6,000 ratification bonus, annual wage increases, improved medical and retirement plans, and additional days off. This marks the first labor agreement with these employees, who are part of the Society of Professional Engineering Employees in Aerospace's (SPEEA) Wichita Technical and Professional Unit, and precedes upcoming negotiations with SPEEA's larger bargaining units representing engineers and technical workers.

feed.title: Closing the Cloud Security Gap With Runtime Protection
feed.summary: This article details an upcoming webinar by Dynatrace experts on closing the cloud security gap using runtime protection. The webinar will explain why traditional pre-runtime security methods are insufficient and how runtime visibility can help detect and prioritize real threats in live cloud-native environments. It aims to demonstrate how these insights can improve collaboration and outcomes for Security, DevOps, and Platform teams.

feed.title: Genasys sets 2026 compensation plan for CEO Richard Danforth
feed.summary: Genasys Inc. has approved the 2026 fiscal year compensation package for CEO Richard Danforth, which includes a base salary of $490,000 and a potential cash bonus tied to revenue, annual recurring revenue (ARR), and debt repayment targets. The package also features 400,000 restricted stock units (RSUs), split between time-based and performance-based vesting, while previous performance-based options were canceled. This announcement comes amid financial challenges for the company and follows recent news of a larger-than-expected loss per share in Q4 2025 and progress on a $75 million emergency warning system project.

feed.title: LM Funding America secures $11 million loan renewal with Galaxy Digital
feed.summary: LM Funding America (NASDAQ:LMFA) has renewed an $11 million loan agreement with Galaxy Digital LLC, according to an SEC filing. This new loan, part of an existing Master Digital Currency Loan Agreement, was completed on Wednesday and used to repay a previous loan from October 2025. The loan is secured by Bitcoin collateral and matures on February 27, 2026.

feed.title: Fortis price target raised to $49 from $46 at BofA
feed.summary: BofA has increased its price target for Fortis (FTS) to $49 from $46, while maintaining an Underperform rating. The adjustment comes after rolling the firm's valuation year forward and expecting Fortis to report a rise in Q4 operating EPS to C$0.85, driven by rate base growth and execution.

feed.title: Northrop Grumman awarded $50 million defense software contract By Investing.com
feed.summary: Northrop Grumman Systems Corp. has been awarded a $50 million contract by the U.S. Department of War for commercial agile software development services. The contract, a time-and-materials, firm-fixed-price task order, involves the sustainment and modernization of software for the Strategy Mission Planning and Execution, Data Fusion, and Visualization suite of applications. The work is scheduled to be completed by October 31, 2031, at Bellevue and Offutt Air Force Base in Nebraska.

feed.title: Caledonia Announces Proposed $100 Million Offering of Convertible Senior Notes Due 2033
feed.summary: Caledonia Mining Corporation Plc has announced its intention to offer $100 million in Convertible Senior Notes due 2033 through a private placement. The company also plans to grant initial purchasers an option to acquire an additional $20 million in notes. Proceeds from this offering will primarily fund capped call transactions, provide financial flexibility, and support the development of the Bilboes gold project in Zimbabwe, alongside other general corporate and operational needs.

feed.title: Becton Dickinson shareholders approve equity plan amendment and executive severance plan
feed.summary: Becton Dickinson (NYSE: BDX) shareholders approved an amendment to the company's 2004 Employee and Director Equity-Based Compensation Plan, increasing shares available for awards by 3,935,000, and also ratified the appointment of Ernst & Young as their independent auditor. The Board of Directors also approved an executive severance plan for management employees in specific job levels if terminated without cause, which includes cash payments, bonuses, medical benefits, and outplacement services. This article also details other recent news for BD, including a quarterly dividend, stock repurchases, and collaborations.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley co-president Simkowitz sells $6m in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley Co-President Daniel A. Simkowitz sold 32,968 shares of common stock for approximately $6 million on January 30, 2026, at a weighted average price of $182.6108. Following this transaction, Simkowitz directly owns over 357,000 shares and indirectly owns additional shares through a 401(k) plan. This news comes amidst other significant activities involving Morgan Stanley, including a raised price target from RBC Capital and involvement in potential IPOs for Jersey Mike’s Subs and L’Occitane Groupe.

feed.title: Curet, Intuitive Surgical EVP, sells $65k in shares
feed.summary: Myriam Curet, EVP and Chief Medical Officer at Intuitive Surgical Inc (NASDAQ:ISRG), sold 126 shares for approximately $65,522. This transaction, executed under a pre-arranged trading plan, occurred after Curet also exercised options to purchase an equivalent number of shares. In other news, the company reported strong Q4 2025 results and positive analyst ratings following the rollout of its da Vinci 5 surgical system.

feed.title: Bank of America stock: 3 things that could move BAC after Friday’s close
feed.summary: Bank of America (BAC) shares closed up 0.23% at $53.20, with little after-hours movement. The stock's future performance is heavily influenced by interest rate outlooks, particularly after President Trump nominated Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair and new inflation data emerged. Upcoming catalysts include the U.S. jobs report on Feb. 6 and the continued market reaction to monetary policy expectations and bond yields.

feed.title: Intuit stock price: TurboTax maker lines up $5.8B tax-season credit line — what to watch next week
feed.summary: Intuit (INTU) shares dropped 0.8% after the company disclosed a $5.8 billion short-term revolving credit facility to fund its early tax-refund program, maturing on March 31. This credit line, which Intuit hasn't yet drawn on, is crucial as the tax filing season peaks and could be influenced by increased demand for early refunds and funding costs. The company is set to release its fiscal second-quarter results on February 26, which investors will closely watch for insights into customer demand and any impact of the credit facility.

feed.title: METC CLASS ACTION NOTICE: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz Files Securities Fraud Lawsuit Against Ramaco Resources, Inc.
feed.summary: The Law Offices of Frank R. Cruz has filed a securities fraud class action lawsuit against Ramaco Resources, Inc. (NASDAQ: METC) on behalf of investors who purchased shares between July 31, 2025, and October 23, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Ramaco made misleading statements regarding its Brook Mine, which was reportedly a "hoax" with no active mining operations. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to seek lead plaintiff status by the specified deadline.

feed.title: Delek US Holdings to Host Fourth Quarter 2025 Conference Call on February 27th
feed.summary: Delek US Holdings, Inc. announced that it will release its fourth quarter 2025 results before the U.S. stock market opens on February 27, 2026. A conference call to discuss these results is scheduled for 10:00 a.m. CT (11:00 a.m. ET) on the same day. The broadcast will be available on the company's investor relations website, with a replay accessible for 90 days.

feed.title: Minority investors back Luis Felipe Henao for Ecopetrol board seat
feed.summary: Ecopetrol (NYSE: EC) announced that its minority shareholders have nominated Luis Felipe Henao Cardona for a board seat. He will be considered as the ninth director on the slate proposed by the Government of Colombia for the upcoming shareholder election. Ecopetrol, a major integrated energy company in the Americas, highlighted its significant operations in Colombia and regional presence, along with forward-looking risk factors.

feed.title: Sprott Eric buys Hycroft Mining (HYMC) shares worth $9.19 million By Investing.com
feed.summary: Eric Sprott, a ten percent owner of Hycroft Mining (HYMC), purchased 200,000 shares for $9.19 million on January 29, 2026, despite a recent 26% share price decline. This purchase increases his total holdings to over 36.7 million shares. This insider activity follows Hycroft Mining's reports of record-breaking silver grades at its Nevada mine and corporate governance developments.

feed.title: Better Energy Stock: Canadian Natural Resources vs. Brookfield Renewable Partners
feed.summary: This article compares Canadian Natural Resources (CNQ) and Brookfield Renewable Partners (BEP) as energy investment options. Both companies demonstrate strong performance, with CNQ focusing on traditional oil and gas and BEP on renewable and nuclear energy. The analysis highlights their financial results, dividend consistency, and future growth potential to help investors decide between immediate returns from traditional energy or transformational growth from clean energy.

feed.title: AngloGold Ashanti Up 7% Last Week After Gold Prices Hit Record Levels
feed.summary: AngloGold Ashanti (AU) stock saw a 7% increase last week, influenced by volatile gold prices that surged above $5,300 before falling below $5,000 per ounce. Despite these swings, the company's fundamentals remained unchanged, with market attention focused on macro-driven expectations. A valuation model suggests a target price of $138.95, implying a potential 29.8% total upside and 14.6% annualized returns, contingent on sustained operating leverage and margin realization.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Enova International (ENVA) After Its Strong Five-Year Run?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Enova International's (ENVA) valuation after a significant five-year stock run, finding mixed results. An Excess Returns model suggests the stock is overvalued at US$165.17, with an intrinsic value of US$82.91. However, a Price vs. Earnings analysis indicates the stock might be undervalued, trading at a P/E of 13.24x compared to Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" of 17.10x.

feed.title: Baird raises Regeneron Pharma stock price target to $742 on earnings beat
feed.summary: Baird has increased its price target for Regeneron Pharma (NASDAQ:REGN) to $742 from $630, maintaining a Neutral rating, following the company's strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings report that surpassed analyst expectations. Key drivers for the positive outlook include the performance of Eylea HD and Dupixent, with several catalysts anticipated in 2026, such as new combination therapies and planned Phase 3 trials. Other analysts, including Bernstein, Evercore ISI, and BofA Securities, have also adjusted their price targets for Regeneron, reflecting a mixed but generally optimistic view of the company's future prospects despite some competitive concerns.

feed.title: JD.Com Signs Strategic Partnership With CBBC
feed.summary: JD.Com has signed a strategic partnership with CBBC. The article, which is less than a minute read, was published by Refinitiv and focuses on Chinese stocks, strategy, business, and products.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser Q4 2025 slides: Mixed results amid strategic transition to 2030 growth plan
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (NYSE:WY) reported mixed Q4 2025 results, with GAAP earnings beating expectations due to special items but underlying performance showing a loss. The company faced challenges in its Timberlands and Wood Products segments, while Real Estate, Energy & Natural Resources performed well. Despite current market headwinds, Weyerhaeuser initiated an ambitious 2030 growth strategy aiming for $1.5 billion in incremental Adjusted EBITDA.

feed.title: Gladstone Investment Announces Monthly Cash Distributions for January, February and March 2026, and Earnings Release and Conference Call Dates for the Third Fiscal Quarter
feed.summary: Gladstone Investment Corporation (NASDAQ:GAIN) has announced monthly cash distributions of $0.08 per share for January, February, and March 2026, totaling $0.24 for the quarter. The company will also report its third fiscal quarter earnings for the period ending December 31, 2025, after the market closes on February 3, 2026, and will host a conference call on February 4, 2026, to discuss the results. Investors can find details about the dividend reinvestment plan and conference call access on the company's website.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Hilltop Holdings Q4 2025 earnings beat expectations
feed.summary: Hilltop Holdings (HTH) reported strong Q4 2025 financial results, with EPS of $0.69 and revenue of $329.89 million, significantly exceeding analyst expectations. The company's net income for the full year 2025 increased by 46% to $165.6 million. Following the announcement, Hilltop's stock price rose by 2.61% in premarket trading, reflecting investor confidence in its diverse business model and strategic growth initiatives.

feed.title: Frances Arnold Sells 102 Shares of Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) Stock
feed.summary: Frances Arnold, a director at Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG), sold 102 shares of the company's stock on January 29th at an average price of $340, reducing her total holdings to 18,307 shares. This transaction follows previous sales in December and comes as Alphabet reported strong Q4 earnings, declared a quarterly dividend, and faces both positive AI developments and regulatory challenges. Analysts maintain a "Buy" consensus rating for Alphabet with a target price of $330.83.

feed.title: Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Major Shareholder Sells $35,060,852.00 in Stock
feed.summary: A major Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) shareholder, 2017 Gp L.L.C. Gv, sold 1,845,308 shares of the company's stock on January 30th for a total value of $35,060,852.00. This transaction was disclosed in an SEC filing and occurred while Alphabet's shares traded down 0.1% to $338.00. The company recently reported strong Q3 earnings, exceeding analyst expectations, and has several key developments in AI and autonomous vehicles, alongside facing regulatory and IP risks.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc: director Arnold sells $34,680 in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Frances Arnold, a director at Alphabet Inc (NASDAQ:GOOGL), sold 102 shares of Class C Capital Stock for $34,680 on January 29, 2026, when the stock was trading near its 52-week high. Following the sale, Arnold directly holds 18,307 shares and various Google Stock Units with staggered vesting schedules. The transaction was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To HP (HPQ) Dividend Payout As Greenlight Capital Exits Over AI PC Risks
feed.summary: HP Inc. recently announced a US$0.30 per-share dividend, its second for fiscal 2026, amidst a challenging investment landscape. Greenlight Capital exited its HP position, citing rising memory costs and weak AI-driven PC demand as significant risks. This article explores how these factors, alongside differing fair value estimates among investors, shape HP's current investment narrative.
